finding_results.notes: Removed the narrator-applause line ('The line landed because it was true and because Nate would have thought the same thing about someone else.'). Ray's stairwell insult at line 345 now flows directly into Nate's concession at line 347 ('I know,' he said.) with only a paragraph break between them. The sting registers through the immediate, unqualified concession — no narration evaluates how well the line played. Ray's wording is preserved unchanged because it is already coworker-specific and off-the-cuff; the defect was solely in the narrator's certification of the hit, not in the dialogue itself.

summary: Single finding addressed: removed narrator commentary that admired Ray's dialogue ('The line landed...') from the stairwell confrontation. The fix lets Ray's insult and Nate's immediate 'I know' carry the exchange without authorial applause. Additionally fixed an internal continuity error (green ink described as 'red streak' at former line 587, now corrected to 'green streak' at line 585) to match the established ink color from lines 471/565. All character dialogue registers verified against style bible contraction levels (Victor moderate, Ray high, Nate high, Sarah high) — no adjustments needed. No other narrator-admires-dialogue patterns found in the chapter.
